notes_md:
**từ** means “word” — a single unit of language with meaning.

It’s a noun. You can say *một từ*, *hai từ* (no special classifier needed). Very common pairings: *từ mới* (new word), *từ khó* (difficult word), *từ vựng* (vocabulary).

It can also mean “from” (starting point in time or place), and this is extremely common.


Think: **từ = word**, but also **từ = from** — two super high-frequency uses.

example.vi: Tôi học từ mới mỗi ngày.
example.es: Aprendo palabras nuevas todos los días.
example.en: I learn new words every day.

definitions.es: Una palabra o término utilizado para referirse a algo.
definitions.vi: Một từ hoặc thuật ngữ được sử dụng để chỉ một cái gì đó.
definitions.en: A word or term used to refer to something.

notes:
từ means “word” — a single unit of language with meaning.

It’s a noun. You can say một từ, hai từ (no special classifier needed). Very common pairings: từ mới (new word), từ khó (difficult word), từ vựng (vocabulary).

It can also mean “from” (starting point in time or place), and this is extremely common.

Think: từ = word, but also từ = from — two super high-frequency uses.

translation.en: I need to understand this word.
